title: GET Device List and Print Hostnames

problem.statement: A mock inventory API's GET /api/devices returns the JSON shown below, already parsed into a Python dict. Fill in the missing return statement for get_hostnames so it returns a list of hostnames.
problem.use_case: This is the exact shape of code you write right after requests.get(url).json() in any real inventory-sync script — pulling one field out of a list of nested objects.

hints: Access response_json['devices'] (or .get('devices', []) to handle a missing key safely).
A list comprehension pulling out 'hostname' per item does this in one line.

solution.explanation: A list comprehension over the 'devices' array extracts the hostname field: return [d['hostname'] for d in response_json.get('devices', [])].
